reasoning: The presidential decree dissolving all political parties was issued on May 13, 2025. The deadline for reopening them is July 1, 2025, a window of less than seven weeks. Military juntas rarely reverse such drastic power-consolidation decrees within merely a few weeks, as doing so would signal significant weakness. Mali's current junta under Assimi Goïta has consistently defied international pressure (e.g., leaving ECOWAS, expelling MINUSMA, repeatedly delaying elections) and is highly unlikely to capitulate to pressure to reverse this decree so quickly. For these reasons, the probability of a reversal before July 1, 2025, is assessed to be very low.
